title: Memory Boxes - Variables
description: Learn to store and use data with variables - the foundation of managing information in code.

preDialogues.name: Maru
preDialogues.content: Hello! I'll teach you about variables!

preDialogues.name: Maru
preDialogues.content: Think of variables as labeled boxes. You can put information inside them and retrieve it later. They're how computers remember things!

preDialogues.name: Qubit
preDialogues.content: So like... a storage container with a name tag?

preDialogues.name: Maru
preDialogues.content: Perfect analogy! Let's create your first variable.

preDialogues.name: Maru
preDialogues.content: To create a variable, give it a name and assign it a value using the equals sign. Let's create a variable called wheat_count and set it to 0!

preDialogues.name: Qubit
preDialogues.content: The equals sign puts the value in the box?

preDialogues.name: Maru
preDialogues.content: Exactly! The equals sign is called the assignment operator. It stores the value on the right into the variable on the left.

postDialogues.name: Maru
postDialogues.content: Great! You've created your first variable. Now that wheat_count exists, you can use it in your code.

preDialogues.name: Maru
preDialogues.content: Variables aren't useful unless you use them! You can reference wheat_count anywhere in your code to get its value.

preDialogues.name: Qubit
preDialogues.content: So if I write wheat_count later, it will have the value I stored?

preDialogues.name: Maru
preDialogues.content: Precisely! Variables persist throughout your program. Try using your variable in the code.

objectives.description: Run code that uses your variable

postDialogues.name: Maru
postDialogues.content: Perfect! Your code remembered the value you stored. That's the power of variables!

preDialogues.name: Maru
preDialogues.content: Variables can change! If you harvest wheat, you want to track that by updating the variable's value.

preDialogues.name: Qubit
preDialogues.content: How do I update a variable?

preDialogues.name: Maru
preDialogues.content: You can take the current value, add to it, and store the result back! Try making wheat_count equal to itself plus 1.

preDialogues.name: Qubit
preDialogues.content: Oh! So it's like updating what's in the box. The old value gets replaced.

preDialogues.name: Maru
preDialogues.content: Exactly! This is called incrementing. It's very useful for tracking counts!

postDialogues.name: Maru
postDialogues.content: Excellent! You've learned to create, use, and update variables. These are the building blocks of data management!

postDialogues.name: Qubit
postDialogues.content: I can see how this would be useful for tracking harvests, planted crops, and more!

postDialogues.name: Maru
postDialogues.content: Precisely! Next, we'll explore how to use variables for counting and arithmetic.
